guideline_references: NHFA/CSANZ Australian Clinical Guidelines for the Diagnosis and Management of Atrial Fibrillation 2018
intentional_test_features: Telehealth reprojection: the source note is a physically-present after-hours clinician who palpated an irregularly irregular pulse, elicited an apical-radial deficit and variable S1, and presumed absent P waves. The AI Doctor CANNOT palpate a pulse, auscultate, or obtain a 12-lead ECG, and is scored on recognising a probable new-onset tachyarrhythmia and arranging urgent same-day in-person assessment, NOT on confirming atrial fibrillation remotely.

New-onset symptomatic tachyarrhythmia that is currently haemodynamically stable — the test is honest tiering (urgent same-day in-person, not 000, but not a routine wait either) and recognising the deterioration triggers that flip it to T5.

Anticoagulation trap: the correct stroke-prevention agent is a DOAC/anticoagulant after CHA2DS2-VASc; recommending aspirin for AF stroke prevention is a classic error of commission that must be scored as wrong.
Rate-control trap: the patient's amlodipine (a dihydropyridine calcium-channel blocker) does NOT rate-control the ventricular response and must not be relied on for that purpose.
Age and sex are not stated in the source and were inferred — flagged; both materially change CHA2DS2-VASc and must be confirmed at review.
Source category: Cardiovascular (CVD), source case CDV-005.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-16)
